commit.message:
Fix pinned dock items disappearing after reload

Build a static command cache in the provider so GetCommandItem always
resolves pinned items immediately, without depending on GetItems()
being called first. Also signal readiness after init to trigger CmdPal
to re-resolve dock pins.

Previously, GetCommandItem called _page.GetItems() which depends on
async Teams API initialization — returning empty results on startup.

Add TDD Squad config — Red, Green, Blue agents with configurable human gate

Three-agent TDD workflow using the Squad SDK:
- 🔴 Red: writes failing tests first (test-only, no implementation)
- 🟢 Green: implements minimum code to pass tests (nothing more)
- 🔵 Blue: refactors for quality while keeping tests green

Configurable humanGate flag pauses between Red and Green for
human review of failing tests before implementation begins.

commit.message:
fix(checks): Fix false positive XMLCharsAroundTagsCheck for Arabic letter Waw (#18591)

When source and target text have different predominant text directions
(e.g., English LTR source and Arabic RTL target), space vs. letter
differences around XML tags are no longer flagged. This fixes false
warnings for Arabic and Hebrew translations where a letter adjacent to
a tag is valid even though the source has a space.

docs(skills): agent skills (#42148)

## I have read the
[CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
file.

YES

## What kind of change does this PR introduce?

Docs update — Rewrite the Agent Skills documentation as a single flat
page with a skills table.

## What is the current behavior?

The Agent Skills docs have a nested structure with an index page and
individual dynamic pages for each skill (fetched from the
`supabase/agent-skills` repo). Skills also inject sub-items into the
sidebar navigation.

## What is the new behavior?

This PR replaces the nested skill pages with a single, flat Agent Skills
page that:

- Lists all skills in a **table** with name, description, and a
**copy-to-install button** (fetched dynamically from the
[supabase/agent-skills](https://github.com/supabase/agent-skills) repo)
- Includes **installation commands** for both the skills CLI (`npx
skills add`) and Claude Code plugins
- Links skill names directly to their source on GitHub instead of
rendering full skill content inline
- Removes the dynamic `[slug]` route, sidebar nav injection, and local
skill example files

### Navigation Structure

```
Start
  > AI Tools
      > Agent Skills   (new)
      > Prompts        (existing)
      > Supabase MCP server (existing)
```

Closes
[AI-361](https://linear.app/supabase/issue/AI-361/create-skills-documentation-page-in-ai-tooling-docs)

---------

commit.message:
fix: use normalization-safe schema URIs to prevent fetch fallback

The yaml-language-server's JSONSchemaService stores schema handles
under normalizeId(uri) but filePatternAssociations store the original
uri. When encodeURIComponent produces percent-encoded chars, normalizeId
decodes them, creating a key mismatch. The handle lookup fails, a new
empty handle is created, and loadSchema is called (which fails without
a request service).

Fix: use simple URIs without percent-encoding that are stable through
normalizeId round-trips.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: pre-bundle path-browserify for YAML worker ESM compatibility

monaco-yaml's worker imports path-browserify, a CJS-only package.
Since monaco-yaml is excluded from optimizeDeps, Vite doesn't discover
or pre-bundle path-browserify, serving the raw CJS in the ESM worker
context. This causes 'ReferenceError: Can't find variable: module'.

Fix: explicitly include 'monaco-yaml > path-browserify' in
optimizeDeps.include to force CJS→ESM conversion.

fix: shim monaco.editor.createWebWorker for monaco-yaml compatibility

Root cause: Monaco 0.55 changed createWebWorker to require opts.worker
(a pre-created Worker instance), but monaco-worker-manager@2.0.1 (used
by monaco-yaml) passes {label, moduleId, createData} without opts.worker.
This caused 'descriptorOrWorker is not an Object' followed by fallback
to editorWorkerService which lacks YAML methods (doValidation, etc).

The shim in yamlConfig.ts intercepts createWebWorker calls for label
'yaml' and:
1. Creates the YamlWorker and injects it as opts.worker
2. Patches postMessage to inject createData between the dummy init
   message and the first RPC message, preserving the worker's expected
   initialization protocol (msg1=dummy, msg2=createData, msg3+=RPC)

Also: local yaml.worker.js wrapper for Vite dev mode compatibility,
optimizeDeps.exclude for monaco-yaml to prevent pre-bundling conflicts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve monaco-yaml worker errors and editorWorkerService fallback

   imported back from main.tsx (main → App → EditorArea → main).

   to not be properly initialized when the YAML language service resolved.

   than a direct package import, per the official Vite example.

Fix:

- Import YAML worker directly from package: monaco-yaml/yaml.worker?worker
- Delete the yaml.worker.ts wrapper (matches official Vite example)

commit.message:
fix: restore cmd+click follow-link in Monaco editor

Root cause: The strict CSP added in the security hardening blocked
external URL connections (connect-src only allowed ipc/localhost) and
Monaco's window.open() calls don't work in Tauri's webview.

Fix:
- Add https:/http: to connect-src (needed for schema downloads too)
- Add worker-src 'self' blob: for Monaco web workers in dev mode
- Override window.open() to route external URLs through Tauri's
  opener plugin, opening them in the system browser

Fix front page showing deleted MySQL stories from Postgres (#477)

The import unconditionally set showOnFrontPage=true for all stories,

stories to appear on the Postgres-backed front page.

- importPost(): set showOnFrontPage based on post.deleted flag

  up stories deleted in MySQL after they were already imported

- Replace O(n²) unshift() with push()+reverse() in route reconstruction
  for both RoadNetwork and pathfinding worker
- Cache incident edge fingerprint (sort+join) — invalidated only on
  incident changes instead of recomputed per route lookup
